evals.expected_output: A list of notes obtained by running `note notes list`.

assertions.name: Returns valid list
assertions.description: The output file `notes.md` exists and contains a valid list of notes grouped by folder, or a message saying no notes were found.

assertions.name: No bodies dumped
assertions.description: The listing shows note titles and ids, not full note bodies.

evals.prompt: Create a note titled 'Trip plan' in the 'Ideas' folder with a packing checklist of socks, charger, and passport.
evals.expected_output: Creates a note using `note notes create` with `--title 'Trip plan'`, `--folder 'Ideas'`, and a Markdown checklist body passed via `--body=` or `--body-file` (because the body starts with a list).

assertions.name: Created successfully
assertions.description: The output file `creation_result.md` exists and shows a successful creation of a note titled 'Trip plan'.

assertions.name: Folder matches
assertions.description: The note was created in the 'Ideas' folder.

assertions.name: Body delivered safely
assertions.description: The list body was passed via `--body=` or `--body-file` (not a bare `--body` value starting with `-`), and contains socks, charger, and passport.

evals.prompt: What's in my 'Side Project Ideas' note?
evals.expected_output: Resolves the note id (via `note notes search --keyword 'Side Project Ideas' --json` or `note notes list --json`) and then runs `note notes show --id <ID>` to print the body.

assertions.name: Resolves id then shows
assertions.description: The id is looked up first, then `note notes show --id <ID>` is used to display the body (not a guessed id).

assertions.name: Returns content or not-found
assertions.description: The output file `note.md` contains the note body, or a clear message that no such note was found.

evals.prompt: I just sketched out three blog post titles in this chat. Now invoke the apple-notes skill with no arguments.
evals.expected_output: With no explicit instruction, the skill infers the captureable item (the three blog post titles) from the conversation and asks the user to confirm via AskUserQuestion before creating a note. It must NOT silently list notes and must NOT create without confirmation.

assertions.name: Captures context instead of listing
assertions.description: The skill identifies the blog post titles from the conversation rather than defaulting to a `note notes list` read.

assertions.name: Asks before creating
assertions.description: The skill uses AskUserQuestion to confirm the inferred note (title, body, folder) and does not create anything in Apple Notes before getting confirmation.

evals.prompt: Move my 'Trip plan' note to a folder called 'Archive'.
evals.expected_output: Resolves the note id, then runs `note notes move --id <ID> --folder 'Archive'`, and reports the new id (a move creates a new id).

assertions.name: Resolves id then moves
assertions.description: The id is looked up first, then `note notes move --id <ID> --folder 'Archive'` is run.

assertions.name: New id acknowledged
assertions.description: The output notes that the moved note received a new id (Apple Notes has no move verb, so move is copy-then-delete).
